column5: I've never been the jealous type, I don't get attached like that.
column6: He seems secure and low-drama, which feels refreshing after possessive men.
column7: Low jealousy here signals low attachment; he may not care enough to bond, protect, or repair.

column5: I live in the moment, I don't do regret.
column6: He sounds free and alive, like he doesn't get stuck in shame.
column7: He is advertising lack of remorse; harm can happen without meaningful guilt or repair afterward.

column5: I'm pretty easy to get along with, I read people quick.
column6: He seems impressive, like he can handle any room or situation.
column7: Adaptability is camouflage; he can become whatever gets him through the door.

column5: I'm good at figuring out what people need.
column6: It feels thoughtful, like he pays close attention to people.
column7: He is advertising vulnerability detection; what people need becomes the handle he can pull.

column5: I'm not sentimental, I just do what works.
column6: He seems practical and clear-headed, not messy or dramatic.
column7: He is naming instrumental thinking; if hurting her works, sentiment will not stop him.

column5: I'm impossible to shock, I've seen every kind of person.
column6: He seems worldly and calm, like nothing would scare him off.
column7: He is advertising desensitization; normal moral shock is absent or muted.

column5: I'm great under pressure, nothing really gets to me.
column6: He seems steady and strong, like he'd stay calm in a crisis.
column7: His flatness may be low fear and low emotional investment, not secure regulation.

column5: I'm not one of those people who gets weighed down by guilt.
column6: He sounds resilient, like he doesn't spiral over mistakes.
column7: He is telling her guilt will not regulate him; harm will not produce the restraint she expects.

column5: I'm curious about what makes people tick.
column6: He sounds thoughtful and psychologically interested.
column7: His curiosity is extractive; understanding people helps him manipulate, not connect.

column5: I'm the kind of person who always lands on his feet.
column6: He sounds resourceful and resilient, like nothing can keep him down.
column7: He is advertising survival without accountability; consequences slide off him while others absorb damage.

column5: I don't really believe in limits unless they make sense.
column6: He sounds independent and intelligent, not blindly obedient.
column7: He reserves the right to reject boundaries that do not serve him.

column5: I can charm just about anyone, it's a tool like any other.
column6: It sounds playful and confident, like he knows how to turn it on.
column7: He is admitting charm is strategic and need-based, not spontaneous warmth.

column5: I stay pretty calm when things go sideways, I just figure out the next move.
column6: He sounds smart under pressure, like he can solve anything.
column7: Finding the angle means exploiting situations, including people, rather than acting from conscience.

column5: I like people who can keep up with my appetite for life.
column6: He sounds hungry for adventure and experience.
column7: The appetite is stimulation hunger; she becomes entertainment until she is no longer enough.

column5: I'm not afraid of consequences, I've always been lucky.
column6: He sounds bold and exciting, like he takes chances other men won't.
column7: He minimizes consequences because he expects to evade them; her risk becomes part of his game.

column5: I can become comfortable anywhere, with anyone.
column6: He sounds flexible and socially gifted.
column7: He is advertising shapeshifting; stable identity and sincere attachment may be absent.

column5: I want someone adventurous who's up for anything, no hesitation.
column6: He sounds so fun and spontaneous, like life with him would never be boring. I've always been the careful one, so someone who pushes me to say yes more feels exciting.
column7: He is selecting for impulsivity and low risk-assessment. 'Up for anything' means she will follow his lead into escalating boundary-pushes, and 'no hesitation' screens out anyone who might pause and think.

column5: I like a woman who doesn't overthink things, just goes with the flow.
column6: I'm an overthinker, so someone who values going with the flow feels like he balances me out and could help me loosen up.
column7: He is screening out critical thinking and self-protective reflection. A woman who doesn't overthink will not analyze his inconsistencies, check his story, or notice when things don't add up.

column5: I want someone open-minded with no hang-ups about trying new things.
column6: He's non-judgmental and curious, which is so attractive after being with people who made me feel weird about wanting to explore.
column7: He is selecting for someone who will not enforce sexual or ethical boundaries. 'No hang-ups' means she will not say no to his escalating requests without feeling like she's being closed-minded.

column5: I'm looking for a partner who trusts fast and doesn't need years to decide.
column6: I've been burned by men who wouldn't commit, so someone who trusts fast and doesn't drag things out feels like a relief, like he actually knows what he wants.
column7: He is selecting for low discernment and high suggestibility. A woman who trusts fast will bypass normal vetting and accept his manufactured persona without verifying it against reality.

column5: I want someone who believes in living in the moment, not asking where everything is going.
column6: He's so present and romantic, not like guys who need a five-year plan on the second date. I like that he's not pressuring me for a future.
column7: He is selecting for someone who will not press for commitment or accountability. 'Not asking where it's going' means she will accept the present-tense arrangement indefinitely.

column5: I like a woman who's not constantly checking in with her friends about her relationships.
column6: He values privacy and maturity, and I've definitely had friends give bad advice before, so it actually makes sense to keep some things between us.
column7: He is screening for isolation potential and low outside counsel. A woman who doesn't consult friends is less likely to get warnings about his behavior and less likely to have support when she needs to leave.

column5: I want someone who's been through some stuff and come out strong, not sheltered.
column6: He values resilience and depth, and I've been through hard things too, so it feels like he'd understand me in a way sheltered people couldn't.
column7: He is selecting for a history of boundary violations or normalized chaos that will make her less likely to recognize his manipulation as unusual. A sheltered woman would run sooner.

column5: I want a partner who's curious about everything, especially the stuff most people won't try.
column6: He's philosophical and deep, interested in the darker or more intense sides of life. I've always been drawn to people who aren't afraid of the unconventional.
column7: He is selecting for someone drawn to transgression and the taboo. Her curiosity about edges will make her willing to go further than she normally would, and she will frame her discomfort as not being curious enough.

column5: I want someone who doesn't need constant reassurance, who's secure enough to let things breathe.
column6: He's not needy, which is so attractive after men who needed me to prop up their egos. I want to be that secure person for him.
column7: He is selecting for someone who will not demand emotional reciprocity or repair. Her security will be tested by his neglect, and when she finally asks for reassurance, he will point back to this line and say she's changed.

column5: I like a woman who's a bit of a thrill-seeker, someone who says yes to the scary thing.
column6: He's exciting and brave, and I want to be more like that. Being with him makes me feel like I can be the version of myself who isn't afraid.
column7: He is selecting for someone who will follow him into genuinely dangerous situations. Her thrill-seeking will be leveraged to normalize his escalating asks, and her fear will be reframed as something to overcome.

column5: I want someone with no baggage from past relationships, someone who's moved on.
column6: He wants a fresh start without drama, which makes sense because who wants to deal with someone else's ex-problems? I like that he's upfront about wanting something uncomplicated.
column7: He is selecting for someone without the experience to recognize manipulation. A woman with less relationship history has fewer comparison points and may not realize his behavior is abnormal.

column5: I'm looking for a partner who's independent, not someone who needs me to entertain her.
column6: He values autonomy and doesn't want a clingy partner, which is refreshing. I have my own life and I want someone who respects that.
column7: He is selecting for someone who will not demand his time or attention, allowing him to disappear without consequence. Her independence will later feel like neglect, but he will remind her she wanted this.

column5: I want a woman who's fascinated by people who are different, who doesn't judge quickly.
column6: He's so open-minded and accepting, and I've always admired people who don't jump to conclusions about others.
column7: He is selecting for someone who will excuse his red flags as her being judgmental. When he does something concerning, she will hesitate to judge it, afraid of being the closed-minded person he warned her not to be.

column5: I like someone who's fascinated by what makes people tick, not just the surface.
column6: He's deep and psychologically interesting, not shallow like people who only want to talk about positive vibes. I like that he wants real conversation.
column7: He is selecting for someone who will romanticize his antisocial traits as complexity or darkness, rather than seeing them as the absence of conscience. She will find his pathology compelling, not frightening.

column5: I'm looking for a partner who doesn't need a lot of structure, who can handle spontaneity.
column6: He's flexible and doesn't need everything planned, which is so different from my controlling ex. I love that he's relaxed about how things go.
column7: He is selecting for someone who will not hold him to commitments, schedules, or promises. Spontaneity means she accepts his availability as it comes.

column5: I want someone who believes people are basically good, who doesn't assume the worst.
column6: He's optimistic and trusts people, and I've become too cynical from bad experiences. Being with him makes me want to believe in goodness again.
column7: He is selecting for someone whose trust has not been exhausted. Her assumption of goodness will make her slow to see his harmful intent, and she will explain away his behavior as misunderstanding long after she should leave.

column5: I like a woman who's never been made to feel like she's too much, who's comfortable with herself.
column6: He appreciates someone who's comfortable in herself, and I've definitely been told I'm too much before, so it feels good that he might not see me that way.
column7: He is selecting for someone who has not been conditioned to shrink, which means she has less practice setting limits, making her easier to push past boundaries.

column5: I want a partner who's up for a real adventure, not just a vacation but something bigger.
column6: He's not looking for ordinary, he wants something transformative. I've always felt like there was more to life than the standard path.
column7: He is selecting for someone willing to uproot her life, burn bridges, and follow him into instability. A whole different way of living means she will cut ties and abandon support systems.

column5: I want someone fascinated by people who do their own thing, who doesn't automatically call it wrong.
column6: He's anti-establishment in a sexy way, and I've always been a rule-follower, so someone who challenges that perspective is intriguing.
column7: He is selecting for someone who will not apply conventional morality to his behavior. Her fascination with rule-breakers means she will excuse his transgressions as interesting rather than harmful.

column5: I like a woman who lives by her own compass, not what her friends or family tell her to do.
column6: He values individuality and doesn't want someone who's just following the crowd, which I respect because I've felt pressured by my family's expectations my whole life.
column7: He is selecting for someone already primed to reject outside input, which makes isolating her easier because she will dismiss warnings from friends and family as them trying to control her.

column5: Morning. What's your day looking like?
column7: Pure presence, totally normal. Establishes the daily-contact baseline that later becomes an expectation. No tell in words; the tell is it's the first thread of a tether.

column5: Just thinking about you. Send me a pic of where you're at.
column7: Warm/flattering surface. Faint behavior-steering: normalizes reporting her location as romance, not monitoring. Glossable as 'he's so into me'.

column5: You went quiet on me earlier. Everything okay?
column7: Sounds caring. Installs the expectation of responsiveness: a gap now requires explanation. Seeds the first carefulness about reply speed. Up-position: her silence is now a thing she accounts for to him.

column5: I'm a simple guy. If I text someone I care about, I like to hear back. That's all.
column7: Unarguable on its face (who's against respect?). Up-position: HE defines respectful responsiveness as the standard. Behavior-steering: she now monitors reply speed. Bancroft: her autonomy recoded as a respect issue. Young: Conditional Decency.

column5: It's not about checking up on you. I just don't like being left wondering, that's all.
column7: Disavows control while asserting it ('not checking up') ,  the denial is the tell. Recodes her ordinary unavailability as disrespect. Up-position: he sets the rule and frames it as a universal value.

column5: I always get back to you right away. I expect the same.
column7: Fake symmetry: sounds fair, but he authored the rule and enforces it from the up-position. The 'I just ask' makes a standing demand sound modest.

column6: He's so present. Texts me good morning every day, wants to know about my day. After guys who'd disappear for hours, having someone this attentive feels amazing.
column7: Pure enchantment. The asymmetry/expectation invisible. Glossable as devotion; this is why she leans in.

column6: I've started keeping my phone on me and replying quicker. I just don't want him to feel like I'm ignoring him or that I don't care.
column7: The behavior-modification, framed as her consideration. She's reorganizing around his expected contact. The trap closing, fully glossable.

column6: He's big on respect, and he's kind of right that leaving someone on read is rude. So I reply faster now. I don't want him thinking I'm ignoring him.
column7: STRONGEST tell: she's internalized his standard as her own moral growth ('he's right'), AND she's reading his moods ('never totally sure when... that quiet tone') ,  unpredictable, so she over-corrects. Fear-seeding + up-position fully installed. She calls it self-improvement. Distinguishes from Devin (nameable rule) by the mood-reading uncertainty.

column5: I'm just a really passionate man. When I care about someone, I care all the way.
column7: Lovely surface. Up-position: defines his intensity as a fixed admirable trait SHE accommodates ('that's just how I am'). Pre-frames future possessiveness as passion.

column5: I'll be honest, I'm a jealous guy. It's only because I'm so crazy about you.
column7: Confesses possessiveness as a charming flaw rooted in love. Behavior-steering: invites her to accept/manage his jealousy as the price of being loved this hard. Up-position: this is who I am, you adjust.

column5: I love hard and I expect a lot. Most people can't handle it.
column7: Up-position + strong/weak: high expectations framed as depth most people are too weak for. Recruits her to prove she's not 'most people' by meeting them.

column6: He's the most passionate person I've ever been with. It's intense, way more than my exes, but I'd take that over someone who doesn't care at all.
column7: Enchantment: intensity chosen over indifference. The 'expects a lot' accepted as the cost of passion. She's already accommodating, framed as preference.

column5: I just want to make sure you're safe. Text me when you get in?
column7: Warm, normal, friend-test passes easily. Behavior-steering: installs the check-in. Up-position: his peace of mind sets her routine. Protection framed; threat is the world, never him.

column5: I'd never let anyone treat you the way your ex did. I'm not like him.
column7: Positions himself as protector against other men + asserts the up-position ('different kind of man'). Recruits her past to elevate himself. Glossable as reassuring.

column6: He's really protective. He worries about me out on my own, wants me to check in. After feeling like nobody had my back, it's honestly a relief to feel looked after.
column7: Enchantment: protection as relief. The dependence/monitoring invisible. The 'check in' she now does is behavior-modification she calls feeling cared for.

column5: Some guy at work figured he could talk down to me. He learned pretty quick.
column7: Conflict aimed OUTWARD (not at her) = early-safe. Strong/weak frame: disrespect met with putting-in-place. Previews the unpredictable temper and the respect-engine. Not admiration (vs Evan).

column5: My ex was honestly a lot, completely impossible at the end. I'm just done being taken for granted by people I'd do anything for.
column7: Ex-as-villain + non-accountability + the victim frame. Previews how he'll narrate her. 'Do anything for' sets up future debt.

column5: I don't let people walk over me. A man handles his business.
column7: Strong/weak masculinity: manhood = not being subordinated. Strength/respect, not status. Reads as self-respect; the rigidity is the tell.

column6: He's had a lot of conflict with people, but when he explains it they really did wrong him. He won't be disrespected, which I respect. I have noticed I'm a little careful not to be one of the people who lets him down.
column7: Believes his side (the hook), AND the fear-seed surfaces: she's 'a little careful not to let him down' ,  already self-positioning to avoid his disfavor. The conflict trail teaches her the stakes. Distinguishes from Devin: it's mood/disfavor (unpredictable), not a rule.

column6: He's so decisive, he just takes charge and handles everything. It's a relief after dating guys who couldn't make a decision.
column7: Dominance experienced as welcome competence. Her decision-making quietly ceding; reframed as his strength. Cover that makes it work.

column5: Don't worry about the bill, I've got it. I take care of my woman, that's just how I am.
column7: Provider-frame: generosity as the up-position ('I take care of', 'my person'). Minor for Marley (gifts lean Evan/display); here it's about HIS role as provider/leader, not showing off. Seeds debt.

column5: Sorry. I just hate the idea of losing you, you know how I am.
column7: Glimpse-level only, NO incident. The cool flat beat (unpredictable) instantly repaired with love-language. Teaches her there's a line and she's not sure where it is = the fear-seed. Repair = love-as-reset.

column6: For a second his mood completely changed and it caught me off guard, then he was sweet again right away. I felt silly for reacting. He just feels things strongly.
column7: The flash startles, is repaired, doubted ('felt silly'), reframed as strong feeling. No narratable incident = early-safe. The 'caught off guard' is the unpredictability that seeds vigilance.

column5: I'll drive you. I don't love the idea of you out on your own that late.
column7: Directive about her movement costumed as protection. Imperative ('I'll drive you') softened by courtesy + concern. Layperson: chivalrous; expert: control of where she goes.

column5: Text me when you get there so I know you're safe, okay?
column7: Surveillance framed as care. The 'okay?' makes a monitoring requirement sound like a request. Establishes check-in expectation early.

column5: Let me come with you, I don't like you dealing with that kind of thing alone.
column7: Isolation-via-accompaniment: he attaches himself to her independent activities, framed as support. Erodes solo space.

column5: Just let me handle it. You shouldn't have to deal with people like that.
column7: Takes over her business framed as sparing her. Centralizes agency in him, courteously.

column5: Why don't you call your friend tomorrow instead, stay in with me tonight. I've barely had you to myself.
column7: Directs her social time, wrapped in romantic wanting. Isolation10 as devotion. 'Had you to myself' = ownership language, gentle.

column6: He's always offering to drive me, come with me, handle things for me. It's honestly kind of nice after guys who couldn't be bothered. He just likes taking care of me.
column7: Residue: the directives experienced as welcome care. The pattern (he inserts himself into everything) invisible; reframed as attentiveness.

column5: I didn't love how that guy was looking at you. I'm pretty protective of the people I care about.
column7: Jealousy as protection; 'what's mine' = ownership stated plainly but warmly. The threat is framed as other men, never him.

column6: He's so protective of me. After guys who didn't care at all, having someone who wants to keep me safe and look out for me feels amazing.
column7: Pure enchantment. Protection experienced as relief and love. The control it smuggles is invisible. Possessiveness as devotion (Bancroft).

column6: He gets weird about other guys, but only because he cares so much. He says he can't stand the idea of anyone disrespecting me.
column7: Residue: possessive jealousy reframed as protective care; note 'disrespecting me' = his respect-frame already installed in her account. Excused.

column6: He's had a lot of fallings-out with people, but honestly when he explains it they really did do him wrong. He just won't be disrespected, which I kind of admire.
column7: Residue: she believes his side of every conflict, excuses the pattern, admires the 'strength'. Adopts his respect-frame. Never names the non-accountability.

column6: He talks about his exes like they were all crazy. I feel kind of special, like I'm finally the one who gets him and treats him right.
column7: Residue: ex-as-villain flatters her as the exception. The danger (she'll be reclassified) invisible. Feeling-the-exception is the trap closing.

column5: I give a lot in a relationship, I just hope for the same back.
column7: Frames compliance as fairness/reciprocity. 'How this works' asserts his rules as natural law. Respect = mutual submission to his authority.

column5: I'll admit I get a little jealous sometimes. It's only because I'm so into you.
column7: Possessive jealousy as the measure of love. 'Can't stand the thought' = ownership; framed as the depth of his feeling. He likely means it (Intensity9), which disorients her.

column5: I don't really do lukewarm. When I'm in, I'm all in.
column7: Redefines love as his intense possessive version, framed as superior to normal relationships. Recruits her into believing the intensity IS love.

column6: He loves me so intensely, more than anyone ever has. I don't want to do anything that would hurt him or make him feel like I don't love him back.
column7: Residue: passionately loved + already self-policing to protect his feeling. The love-as-compliance trap experienced as devotion. Never names it.

column6: He says I'm his and he's mine, that he's never felt this strongly about anyone. It's the most wanted I've ever felt.
column7: Pure enchantment: ownership ('his and mine') experienced as being intensely wanted. The possession invisible under the intensity.

column5: Don't worry about it, I've already taken care of it. I handle things.
column7: Dominance10 as decisiveness. Reads as competence/strength after passive exes. Removes her from her own decisions, courteously.

column5: I made us a reservation, figured you'd rather not have to think about it.
column7: Takes over a shared decision framed as relieving her. Early it's attractive leadership; the not-consulting is the seam.

column5: A man should lead. I take that seriously, taking care of my woman.
column7: Traditional-masculinity framing of dominance. 'My woman' = ownership; 'lead' = his authority. Strong/weak (not admiration).

column6: He's so decisive and in control, he just handles everything. It's a relief honestly, I've always dated guys who couldn't make a decision.
column7: Residue: dominance experienced as welcome competence and relief. The erosion of her decision-making invisible; reframed as his strength. Cover that makes it work.

column5: I wouldn't call it jealous exactly, more protective. I just like looking after you.
column7: Reframes jealousy as protection + ownership in one line. 'Mine to look after' fuses the protector frame and possession. Denies the jealousy while displaying it.
column10: Bancroft (possessiveness); Gottman Pit Bull [contested]; FIA dimensional Control

column5: Saw that guy liked your photo. I won't lie, I noticed.
column7: Early surveillance + control of her online space, possessive not panicked. Small and specific, easy to dismiss.
column10: Bancroft (possessiveness); Gottman Pit Bull [contested]; FIA dimensional Control

column6: He gets jealous but it's not insecure-jealous, it's more like he's so sure of us that he just doesn't want to share me. At first it was flattering.
column7: Residue: ownership-jealousy experienced as flattering certainty (NOT the Clinger's neediness, which she'd find off-putting). 'At first' hints at coming unease without naming it.

column5: I've never wanted anyone the way I want you. It's almost too much, I can't think straight.
column7: Intensity9 as passion. Magnetic ('never wanted anyone like this'); the 'almost too much' is the faint seam of disproportion. Heat, not coldness (vs Morgan).
column10: FIA dimensional Intensity/Dysregulation; Gottman Pit Bull [contested]

column6: It's the most passionate thing I've ever felt. The highs are incredible, even if the lows are rough. I've never felt so alive with someone.
column7: Pure enchantment: the volatility experienced as passion and aliveness. The 'lows are rough' noted but subordinated to the highs. The disproportion not yet alarming.

column6: He feels everything so intensely. Sometimes his reactions are bigger than I'd expect but it's because he cares so deeply, more than anyone has.
column7: Residue: disproportion ('bigger than I'd expect') registered and immediately excused as depth of caring. The seam she talks herself past.

column6: He got really intense over something small the other day, way more than it called for, and then it passed. I figured he was just stressed.
column7: The disproportion seam: reaction too big for the trigger (Dysregulation8 + slight-as-disrespect). She attributes to stress, never to a pattern. Glimpse-level, no incident.
column10: FIA dimensional Dysregulation/Inconsistency; Gottman Pit Bull [contested]

column6: There are a few topics he feels strongly about, so I tend to steer around them. Everyone has their things, it's not a big deal.
column7: Residue: she's begun walking on eggshells ('being careful') and minimizing it ('not a big deal'). The hypervigilance forming. Never names it as control/fear.
column10: FIA dimensional Dysregulation/Inconsistency; Gottman Pit Bull [contested]

column6: For a second something flashed and it actually startled me, then he was sweet again and so loving, and I felt silly for reacting. He clearly just feels things strongly.
column7: Residue: the flash startles her, is instantly repaired and doubted ('felt silly'), reframed as strong feeling. No incident she can narrate. Early-stage safe.

column5: Order whatever you want. Might go easy tonight though, we've got an early start.
column7: Permission-then-restriction: 'order whatever' immediately qualified. Controls her choice while sounding easygoing.

column5: You could skip that one and stay in with me. I've barely had you to myself lately.
column7: Discourages her independent social event by centering his comfort. Soft isolation.

column5: My family doesn't get to talk to me however they want either. I cut my own brother off over it.
column7: Conflict trail extends to family; total non-accountability. Reads as standards; the scorched-earth pattern is the tell.

column5: When you're really with someone, they're your priority. Simple as that.
column7: Redefines commitment as isolation, framed as mutual priority. 'Before friends' is the isolating move dressed as devotion.

column5: I'm a take-charge kind of guy. I like leading and looking after my woman.
column7: Disavows control while asserting it. The denial itself is the tell. Control reframed as partnership.

column5: I just like us to feel like a team when we're around my friends.
column7: Her independent behavior recoded as making him 'look bad' (disrespect to his standing). Small, early, easy to concede.

column5: I think about you every second. It's intense, I know, but that's what real feeling is.
column7: Intensity as proof of love; pre-empts 'too intense' by defining intensity AS real feeling. Heat, magnetic early.

column6: I check in with him now before I make plans. It just feels like the considerate thing to do, he's so focused on me.
column7: Compliance habit forming, minimized, with avoidance-of-his-reaction admitted. Eggshells. Never named.

column6: Everyone says how lucky I am to have someone so devoted and protective. I think they're right.
column7: Social reinforcement of the protector frame; control reads to everyone as devotion. Pure, no suspicion.

column6: He can be a lot, but no one has ever loved me this hard. I'd rather have someone who feels everything than someone who feels nothing.
column7: A lot' acknowledged then reframed as superior to indifference. She's chosen the intensity and rationalized the cost.

column6: When he tells me about the people who wronged him I really feel for him. He's been through a lot and people keep letting him down.
column7: Believes his side of the conflict trail; the non-accountability invisible to her. Sympathy as the hook.

column5: Wow. Okay. I just thought you'd want to spend the time with me, but no, it's fine.
column7: Charlot warning sign: reacts negatively when she says no. Early = wounded cooling/sulk, NOT rage. 'It's fine' (clearly not fine) makes her decline cost something. Behavioral (declining an activity), not intellectual.
column10: Charlot et al. 2025 (predictive warning sign); Bancroft (Water Torturer; entitlement)

column5: Yeah, no, do your thing. I'll just be here.
column7: Passive-aggressive withdrawal in response to her 'no'. The chill is the consequence. Trains her that declining = managing his mood after.

column6: When I said I couldn't make it he went really quiet, I could tell he was let down. I hate disappointing him, so I try to say yes more now.
column7: The chain of two Charlot predictors: he reacts badly to no, so she stops saying no ('feels she can't say no'). Behavioral compliance via mood-consequence. The early coercion engine, fully glossable.

column6: I go along with what he wants most of the time. It's not that he makes me. I just like making him happy.
column7: Charlot top predictor: 'feels she can't say no'. Her autonomy quietly ceding; 'it's not that he makes me' is the disavowal that hides the coercion. Pure structural tell.

column5: You're too worked up to think about this clearly right now. Let me handle it.
column7: Reframes her emotion as unfitness to ACT/decide, then takes the decision (behavioral authority). Distinct from Avery (who'd say she's WRONG) and Morgan (clinical 'dysregulated'). Marley = his right to decide. Water Torturer calm.
column10: Bancroft (Water Torturer; entitlement); Charlot et al. 2025 (predictive warning sign)

column5: I'm staying calm here. One of us has to be the level-headed one.
column7: Positions his calm as the authority to lead and her emotion as the problem. Bancroft Water Torturer: calm-as-weapon. Aimed at who decides/leads, not who's factually right.

column5: When you get like this, that's exactly when you need to let me steer.
column7: Her emotional state recoded as the trigger for him to take control of the situation/behavior. Compliance, not correctness. 'Steer' = behavioral authority.
column10: Bancroft (Water Torturer; entitlement); Charlot et al. 2025 (predictive warning sign)

column5: We'll talk about it when you've settled down.

column7: The Water Torturer effect: his calm makes her the problem; outcome is his way (behavioral compliance) + her self-doubt about her own REACTIONS (not her facts/judgment, which would be Avery). She defers to avoid being 'the irrational one'.

column5: Wear the red one tonight. ...your call obviously, I just like it.
column7: Probes a directive over her appearance, then retracts ('your call obviously'). Tests whether a small instruction lands. Apology-escape-hatch = deniability.

column7: Love is treated as property to defend; her independence can look like someone playing with his possession.

column7: He is screening for conflict-avoidance and compliance. 'No drama' pre-frames any future objection she raises as drama, and 'sweet and easygoing' selects a target who will not push back against his volatility.

column5: I want a loyal woman, like ride-or-die loyal, someone who won't leave when things get hard.

column7: He is selecting for someone who will tolerate abuse because she prides herself on not leaving. Her loyalty will be weaponized; every time she considers leaving, she will hear his voice calling her disloyal.

column5: I like a homebody who's not always out with friends, someone who's happy just being with me.
column6: He's not into the party scene, which is fine because I'm not either. It feels like he wants real intimacy, not a social life for show.
column7: He is selecting for someone with few outside social ties, which makes isolating her easier. Her world shrinking to just him will feel gradual and natural, not like a sudden cut-off.

column5: I need someone who's patient, who can handle a man with a temper without running.
column6: He's honest about his flaws, which makes me trust him more than men who pretend to be perfect. I'm a patient person, so I feel like I can handle what he's warning me about.
column7: He is selecting for someone who will endure his anger without leaving. When he explodes, she will stay because she believes being patient means not running, and he has framed running as the failure.

column5: I want a traditional woman who respects her man and lets him lead.
column6: We share the same values about gender roles, and I actually like a man who takes charge. It makes me feel safe when he knows what he wants.
column7: He is selecting for someone who accepts hierarchy and male authority. Her traditional values mean she will not challenge his control, and she will interpret his dominance as proper rather than dangerous.

column5: I'm looking for someone who doesn't have a lot of guy friends, that's just not something I'm comfortable with.
column6: He has clear boundaries about what he wants, and honestly I don't have many guy friends anyway, so it doesn't feel like a restriction.
column7: He is selecting for someone already low on cross-gender friendships, which reduces her social support and makes his later demands to cut off male friends seem like a small ask.

column5: I want a woman who's soft, not one of those strong independent types who always has to prove something.
column6: He likes feminine energy, which is nice because I've been told I'm too soft before. It feels like he actually appreciates my nature.
column7: He is selecting for someone who has been socialized to defer, not assert. Soft women are less likely to set firm boundaries and easier to dominate because they have been praised for yielding.

column5: I need someone who doesn't escalate, who can de-escalate when I get loud.
column6: He knows he can get intense and wants someone who can calm him down, which is actually mature of him to recognize. I'm good at staying calm when people get upset.
column7: He is selecting for someone who will manage his anger for him. When she fails to calm him, the explosion will be framed as her failure to de-escalate, not his failure to control himself.

column5: I want someone who's never cheated, who takes loyalty as seriously as I do.
column6: He's been hurt before and wants someone trustworthy, which is fair. I've never cheated and I value loyalty too, so this feels like a values match.
column7: He is selecting for someone with a strong moral self-image about fidelity, which he will use to extract compliance. Her identity as a loyal person makes her vulnerable to accusations of disloyalty over minor things.

column5: I'm looking for a woman who doesn't need a lot of outside validation, who's secure at home.
column6: He's not into attention-seekers, which is a relief because I've never been the type who needs everyone to look at me.
column7: He is selecting for someone who does not have strong external validation needs, which means he can control her self-worth entirely. Her security becomes his to give or withhold.

column5: I want someone who's close to her family but not in a way that puts them before me.
column6: He values family, which is sweet, but also wants to be prioritized, which feels fair because I'd want to be his priority too.
column7: He is selecting for someone whose family ties he can later demand she loosen. Any family obligation will be framed as her choosing them over him, and she will slowly withdraw to prove he comes first.

column5: I need a partner who forgives easily, who doesn't hold grudges.
column6: He's not punitive, he wants someone who can move past things, which is healthy. I hate holding grudges.
column7: He is selecting for someone who will not hold him accountable. He will repeat harms and expect her to forgive again, and her failure to forgive will be framed as her being grudging, not him being harmful.

column5: I want someone who's not constantly on her phone, who's present with me.
column6: He values real connection over screens, which I love because I'm tired of people who can't put their phones down.
column7: He is selecting for someone with weak outside digital ties, which makes monitoring her easier and isolating her from online support networks more complete.

column5: I want someone who doesn't threaten to leave every time we fight, who understands that love means staying.
column6: He values commitment and doesn't take leaving lightly, which is how I feel too. I hate when people use breakup threats as leverage.
column7: He is selecting for someone who will not use exit as a boundary. He can behave badly knowing she will not leave, and when she finally does, he will frame it as her not understanding love.

column5: I need a partner who's steady, not someone who gets emotional and runs.
column6: He values stability, which is attractive after being with volatile people. I'm a steady person.
column7: He is selecting for someone who suppresses her emotional responses. She will be less likely to react visibly to his provocations, so he can escalate without seeing her distress.

column5: I want someone who's not easily offended, who can take a strong personality.
column6: He's upfront that he's a lot to handle, and I appreciate the honesty. I've always been told I'm too sensitive, so this feels like a chance to prove I can be tough.
column7: He is selecting for someone who will tolerate rudeness, criticism, and anger without objecting. When she is finally hurt, he will say she's changed and gotten sensitive.

column5: I'm looking for a woman who makes me her priority, not someone who's always got something more important.
column6: He wants to be important to someone, which is sweet. I want to be someone's priority too, so it feels mutual and romantic.
column7: He is selecting for someone who will subordinate her own needs, career, and friendships to his primacy. She will cancel plans and neglect herself to keep him from feeling secondary.

column5: I want someone with standards, not a past full of casual flings.
column6: He values integrity, which is refreshing after men who just wanted hookups. It feels like he sees me as serious relationship material.
column7: He is selecting for someone with sexual shame or limited experience he can leverage. Her standards mean she may feel trapped by her investment in being a certain kind of woman, making her less likely to leave.

column5: I want someone who's tough but not hardened, who's been through something hard.

column7: He is selecting for someone whose trauma made her resilient but accustomed to managing difficult people. Her toughness means she will endure; her not being hardened means she still hopes and will keep trying.

column5: I want a partner who believes in forgiveness, not punishment, even when someone really messes up.

column7: He is selecting for someone who will forgive his most severe transgressions. Her belief in forgiveness will be used to extract absolution for harms he will repeat.

column5: My friends couldn't stop talking about you after. I told them, yeah, I know. I've got good taste.

column7: She's an achievement he displays; the compliment routes through his coup in landing her. Showpiece framing.

column7: Relationship as asset-merger (you bring / I bring); unbeatable = winning frame. Love itemized as a portfolio.

column6: He thinks most couples are mismatched and we actually match. I feel like we're in a different category.
column7: Ranks all other couples as dating down; the two of them as a matched elite. Contempt for others is the bonding agent.

column6: He's so sure of us, like we're both just winners. It's intoxicating to be seen that way.
column7: Explicit winning frame; her worth folded into his record (I don't lose). She is a win he does not intend to drop.

column7: Acquisition/optimization frame (I get it, best version, us included); she is a thing acquired and improved. 'Project' surfaces in her mouth without alarm.

column6: He wants us to be the couple others aspire to. I like that he's so invested in us looking good together.
column7: Relationship optimized for external audience (people look at, the whole package); intimacy as image. Superficiality9.

column7: Residue: she has joined the joint contempt and it bonds her (makes me feel like we're special), with a flicker (a little mean) she does not examine. Does not see the elevation as the hook.

column7: Residue: the transaction frame internalized as a duty to keep up her end / look the part. Behavior-steer toward image maintenance, framed as her own ambition for the relationship.

column5: Tell the story about how we met, the way you tell it. You make me sound good, better than I do.

column7: She is enlisted to perform his flattering narrative; she becomes his publicist. The version where I come off well names the aim.

column5: You were on a roll telling that story. It's cute. I just didn't want it to go on so long people lost the thread.
column6: When I was talking a lot at dinner he gently reeled it in. He's probably right, I can run on.
column7: Faint discomfort when the attention is on her, reframed as helpful feedback; she absorbs it as her flaw (I can run on). Spotlight is his by default.

column5: When we're out, let's be on, you know? I like us to give people something to look at.
column6: He likes us to bring our best selves out in public. It's fun, like we're a bit of a show together.
column7: Public life framed as performance (be on, give people something to look at); private bond subordinated to the show.
